_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Serve as the designated point of contact for supervisory authorities and data subjects exercising their rights, managing inquiries, access requests, erasure requests, and complaints within statutory timeframes.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Task SP-T-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-T-005 (DQ, cosine: 0.448), DQ-S-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.426).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.7/10 (raw 0.570). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [complaint, establish, processes] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, authorities, complaints, contact, data, designated] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.862 (P=0.880, R=0.845). P exceeds R by 0.035 — the NICE element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 27 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Establish complaint processes" and "Serve as the designated point of contact for supervisory authorities and data su" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.455 < STS 0.570 (gap -0.115). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [complaint, establish, processes] and [access, authorities, complaints] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [complaint, establish, processes]. WIDAI terms: [access, authorities, complaints, contact, data, designated].
